commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: This needs contributor-owned Apple Silicon proof plus maintainer product and security scoping; the broad conflicting branch is not a safe automated repair lane.; Needs attention: The diff changes command execution paths and introduces concrete command-wrapper and shell-execution trust-boundary regressions.; Review finding: [P1] Preserve runExec default maxBuffer capacity — src/process/exec.ts:115 - [P1] Keep the tru... (sha=7193619b09299ccfae6af7c9eeb111f7da7c1ea8)
commands.review_summary:
This PR adds automatic Apple Silicon platform utilities, changes process execution, shell selection, and process-tree behavior, and adds hybrid runtime, thread-pool, tensor, docs, and verification files.

Reproducibility: yes. for the blocking PR defects: source inspection of the PR head shows the maxBuffer cap, ComSpec wrapper, detached timeout cleanup, kill-tree, shell-selection, ESM/test, thread-pool, and tensor issues. No high-confidence reproduction exists for the claimed Apple Silicon performance benefit because the PR has no real environment proof.
commands.review_followup: This needs contributor-owned Apple Silicon proof plus maintainer product and security scoping; the broad conflicting branch is not a safe automated repair lane.

target.title: feat: Add Apple Silicon optimizations
target.body:
- Created platform utilities module for ARM64 detection and optimization
- Optimized process execution with larger buffers on ARM64
- Prefers zsh shell on Apple Silicon macOS
- Optimized process tree management with better signal delivery
- Added comprehensive unit tests and documentation

All optimizations are automatic, require no configuration, and are fully backward compatible.

Pushed to: feature/apple-silicon-optimizations branch

## Summary

Describe the problem and fix in 2–5 bullets:

If this PR fixes a plugin beta-release blocker, title it `fix(<plugin-id>): beta blocker - <summary>` and link the matching `Beta blocker: <plugin-name> - <summary>` issue labeled `beta-blocker`. Contributors cannot label PRs, so the title is the PR-side signal for maintainers and automation.

- Problem:
- Why it matters:
- What changed:
- What did NOT change (scope boundary):

## Change Type (select all)

- [ ] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [ ] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #
- Related #
- [ ] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Root Cause / Regression History (if applicable)

For bug fixes or regressions, explain why this happened, not just what changed. Otherwise write `N/A`. If the cause is unclear, write `Unknown`.

- Root cause:
- Missing detection / guardrail:
- Prior context (`git blame`, prior PR, issue, or refactor if known):
- Why this regressed now:
- If unknown, what was ruled out:

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

For bug fixes or regressions, name the smallest reliable test coverage that should have caught this. Otherwise write `N/A`.

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [ ] Unit test
  - [ ] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file:
- Scenario the test should lock in:
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail:
- Existing test that already covers this (if any):
- If no new test is added, why not:

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

List user-visible changes (including defaults/config).  
If none, write `None`.

## Diagram (if applicable)

For UI changes or non-trivial logic flows, include a small ASCII diagram reviewers can scan quickly. Otherwise write `N/A`.

```text
Before:
[user action] -> [old state]

After:
[user action] -> [new state] -> [result]
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`Yes/No`)
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`Yes/No`)
- New/changed network calls? (`Yes/No`)
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`Yes/No`)
- Data access scope changed? (`Yes/No`)
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation:

## Repro + Verification

Attach at least one:

- [ ] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

## Human Verification (required)

What you personally verified (not just CI), and how:

- Verified scenarios:
- Edge cases checked:
- What you did **not** verify:

## Review Conversations

- [ ] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [ ] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

If a bot review conversation is addressed by this PR, resolve that conversation yourself. Do not leave bot review conversation cleanup for maintainers.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Config/env changes? (`Yes/No`)
